summary: CineBoard Social feature testing complete. All backend APIs working correctly. Fixed a bug in like endpoint where films without user_id caused KeyError. Frontend social feed, top-liked, and like toggle all working correctly.

fixed.issue: KeyError: 'user_id' when film has no user_id field
fixed.fix: Changed film['user_id'] to film.get('user_id') with proper null checks

context_for_next_testing_agent: CineBoard Social feature fully tested. Backend endpoints: GET /api/social/feed, GET /api/social/top-liked, GET /api/social/my-bonuses, POST /api/films/{film_id}/like all working. Frontend pages at /social?view=social-feed and /social?view=top-liked working. Like toggle working correctly. Self-like prevention working (returns 400). Dashboard social CTA card present and clickable.

backend: GET /api/social/feed - returns films with like counts, pagination, user_liked flag
GET /api/social/top-liked - returns films sorted by likes_count descending with rank
GET /api/social/my-bonuses - returns likes_given, likes_received, giver_bonus, receiver_bonus
Like bonus formula verified: bonus = log(likes + 1)
frontend: Social feed page loads at /social?view=social-feed with compact grid of films
Each social card shows poster, studio name overlay, like count, quality score
Like button toggles on click (heart fills/unfills, count updates)
Top Liked page loads at /social?view=top-liked with ranked list
Dashboard has pink social CTA card that links to /social?view=social-feed
Self-like shows error toast: 'Non puoi mettere like ai tuoi film!'
